growth_records.strain: natural mixed community (not axenic; monitored via molecular probes for Salinibacter)
growth_records.agit: 225 rpm (Halobacterium strain 004.1 rotary shaker); other halophile growth-rate cultures shaken/rotary
growth_records.medium: Natural crystalliser pond brine (Braç del Port, Alicante, Spain)
growth_records.cite: Stevenson et al. Is there a common water-activity limit for the three domains of life?. The ISME Journal (2015). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2014.219. [PMC4438321]
growth_records.snippet: We determined the water activity of these media...and plotted these values against growth-rate data for this Bacterial assemblage...Growth rates were optimal at 0.841 aw (doubling time=12 h) and reduced by only 50% at 0.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time=12h explicitly reported at optimum water activity (0.841 aw) for the natural Salinibacter-dominated bacterial assemblage monitored in situ; mu calculated as ln2/12h=0.0578 h-1. Community-level measurement (not pure culture), so organism/strain identity is approximate; temperature and oxygen status not stated in the visible text. [metadata completed from paper Methods: temperature, oxygen]
